name: havoc
title: havoc

commands.name: havoc
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# havoc -h
 Havoc Framework [Version: 0.7] [CodeName: Bites The Dust]
 
 Usage:
 havoc [flags]
 havoc [command]
 
 Available Commands:
 client      client command
 help        Help about any command
 server      teamserver command
 
 Flags:
 -h, --help   help for havoc
 
 Use "havoc [command] --help" for more information about a command.

binary_metadata.file_type: POSIX shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.depends: gcc-mingw-w64-i686-win32, gcc-mingw-w64-x86-64-win32, nasm, libc6 (>= 2.38), libgcc-s1 (>= 3.0), libpython3.13 (>= 3.13.0~rc3), libqt5core5t64 (>= 5.15.1), libqt5gui5t64 (>= 5.14.1) | libqt5gui5-gles (>= 5.14.1), libqt5network5t64 (>= 5.0.2), libqt5sql5t64 (>= 5.0.2), libqt5websockets5 (>= 5.6.0), libqt5widgets5t64 (>= 5.14.1), libstdc++6 (>= 13.1)
apt_metadata.description:
Modern and malleable post-exploitation C2 framework
Havoc is a modern, malleable post-exploitation command and control framework
made for penetration testers, red teams, and blue teams.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use havoc because ### havoc.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair havoc with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `havoc --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### havoc.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install havoc
Install with: sudo apt install havoc
